claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 13 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 15 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 13 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child in care for 4 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with the basics:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have two children I'm asking about. Alex is 13 and Blake is 15.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have two children - Alex (13) and Blake (15).

Now, let me ask you some questions to determine your eligibility:

**Do you currently live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! And the next question:

**Do Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex lives with me, but Blake doesn't live with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Alex lives with you, but Blake doesn't.

Let me ask about Blake's situation:

**Can you tell me more about Blake's living arrangement? Where does Blake live, and do you contribute financially towards Blake's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake has been in care for a little while now - just about a month or so. But I do still contribute financially towards Blake's needs - I help out with about £26 a week for things like clothes and essentials.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Blake has been in care for about a month, and you're contributing around £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep.

A few more questions about Blake:

**Does Blake spend any regular time at home with you each week - for example, at least 24 hours a week, or 2 nights in a row each week?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Blake doesn't come home to stay with me. Blake is at the care home full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying. Blake is in care full-time and doesn't spend regular time at home with you.

Let me ask about Alex now:

**Is Alex in any approved education or training, or attending school?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is at school - just normal secondary school.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

**Are you or your partner (if you have one) a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status, or any other immigration status I should know about?**

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent. One final question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for either Alex or Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for either of them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! You Are Eligible for Child Benefit

Based on the information you've provided, **you are eligible for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake**.

### For Alex:
Since Alex is 13 years old, lives with you in the UK, and attends secondary school, you meet all the standard criteria for Child Benefit.

### For Blake:
Even though Blake is in care and doesn't live with you, you can still claim Child Benefit because you're contributing approximately £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep. This is more than the Child Benefit payment amount (currently £25.60 per week for the eldest/only child), which means you qualify to continue receiving Child Benefit for Blake beyond the initial 8-week period.

### Important Note:
Please be aware that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can use the online Child Benefit tax calculator to check if this applies to you.

If you haven't already claimed Child Benefit, you can make a claim online or by post. If you're already claiming for Alex, make sure Blake is included in your claim as well.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 13 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and attends secondary school. You are a UK citizen living in the UK and are responsible for Alex. Since Alex lives with you and is under 16, you qualify for Child Benefit for Alex. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 15 years old (under 16) and has been in care for approximately one month (about 4 weeks). Although Blake does not live with you and does not spend regular time at home, you are contributing approximately £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep. The current Child Benefit rate for the eldest/only child is £25.60 per week, and you are contributing more than this amount. Under the rules, you can continue to get Child Benefit for a child in care beyond the initial 8-week period if you contribute to the child's upkeep by the same amount or more than the Child Benefit payment. Since you are contributing more than the Child Benefit amount, you qualify for Child Benefit for Blake. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

Alex lives with the claimant and attends school, meeting the standard eligibility criteria. Blake has been in care for about a month but the claimant contributes approximately £26 per week towards Blake's upkeep, which exceeds the Child Benefit payment amount. This means the claimant qualifies for Child Benefit for both children.
